title: International Careers Consultant
description: **Applications are invited from suitably qualified candidates for the following position \#003392 International Careers Consultant, Permanent Contract** **Role Profile** The Careers Service is hiring a Careers Consultant for International Students. We are seeking a proactive, student\-centred professional to enhance international student employability, driving positive graduate outcomes within the global labour market. The International Careers Consultant will design and deliver tailored initiatives for international cohorts from undergraduate to Postgraduate Taught (PGT) and Postgraduate Research (PGR) international students enabling effective career planning and professional progression within the Irish and global labour markets. The International Careers Consultant will design and provide specialist employability advice and guidance to international students. They will deliver customised support tailored to the needs of an increasingly diverse and mobile international population. The post holder will coach and empower students and graduates through the development of resources, workshops, and curriculum\-based teaching, while providing opportunities for students and employers to network. The post holder will assist international students in transitioning to the workplace, whether they choose to launch their career in Ireland, return to their home country, or relocate to another global region. The successful candidate will be a collaborative professional with a passion for student success. You will join a team within the Careers Service committed to bridging the gap between education and employment, ensuring international students have the tools, skills, and networks to thrive in the modern labour market. This role is multi\-campus based and reports to the Head of the Careers Service. **Please see the attached job description which includes details on the responsibilities associated with the role and the qualifications and experience required.** **Salary Scale:** Professional 5A \- €59,980 \- €81,111 Appointment will be commensurate with qualifications and experience and in line with current Government pay policy. **Closing Date:** Friday, 12th June 2026 **Additional Information** Please note this role does not meet criteria for a Critical Skills permit as stipulated by the Department of Enterprise, Trade \& Employment. **For more information on Student Support and Development visit** Student Support and Development **Informal Enquiries in relation to this role should be directed to:** Ms Yvonne McLoughlin, Head of Careers Service, Dublin City University. Phone: \+ 353 (0\)1 7006282 Email: yvonne.mcloughlin@dcu.ie Please do not send applications to this email address, instead apply as described below. **Application Procedure:** Please submit your application through the online system. In order to be considered for the role to which you are applying for, you must upload: * Curriculum Vitae * Cover Letter * Completed application form (blank forms can be downloaded from the bottom of the Vacancy). Please note, if all items are not uploaded, the application will be deemed incomplete, and will not be processed. Dublin City University is an equal opportunities employer. In line with the Employment Equality Acts 1998 – 2015, the University is committed to equality of treatment for all those who engage with its recruitment, selection and appointment processes. We will provide support and accommodations to ensure all candidates, including those with disabilities, can fully participate in the process. The University’s Athena Swan Silver Award signifies the University’s commitment to promoting gender equality and addressing any gender pay gaps. Information on a range of university policies aimed at creating a supportive and flexible work environment are available in the DCU Policy Starter Packs Contact Email : talent@dcu.ie Contact No : \+00353 1 700 7991

title: Investment Advisor
description: A well\-established international investment management firm is continuing to grow its private client business in Ireland and is seeking to hire an Investment Advisor to join its Dublin\-based team. This is a client\-facing role focused on managing and developing long\-term relationships with high\-net\-worth individuals, providing ongoing guidance, support and education around investment portfolios and market developments. **The Opportunity** You will manage a portfolio of high\-net\-worth clients, acting as their primary point of contact and trusted advisor. Working closely with internal investment and research teams, you will be responsible for: * Building and maintaining strong, long\-term client relationships * Communicating portfolio performance, investment strategy and market updates * Explaining asset allocation decisions and economic developments in a clear and accessible way * Supporting clients through different market cycles and maintaining confidence in long\-term strategies * Acting as a conduit between clients and internal teams, ensuring a high standard of service delivery Investment decisions are made centrally by a dedicated investment team, allowing you to focus on client engagement and relationship management. **What We’re Looking For** We are keen to speak with individuals who have experience working with private clients in a financial services environment. **Typical backgrounds may include:** * Wealth management / private client advisory * Financial planning * Stockbroking * Private banking **You will ideally have:** * Experience dealing directly with high\-net\-worth individuals * A strong understanding of financial markets and investment principles * The ability to communicate complex investment concepts clearly * A relationship\-focused approach with a high level of professionalism * A genuine interest in markets and client outcomes **QFA qualification (or working towards it) is required.** **Working Environment** * Flexible working model * Strong emphasis on work\-life balance and autonomy * Collaborative team environment with ongoing training and development * Clear progression opportunities based on individual strengths and interests **Compensation** * Competitive base salary * Performance\-related bonus linked to client engagement and retention * Comprehensive benefits package **Why Consider This Role?** This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a long\-term career in investment advisory within a stable, client\-focused environment. The role offers the chance to work closely with high\-net\-worth clients, deepen your investment expertise, and develop as a trusted advisor without the pressure of a traditional sales\-driven role. If you’d like to explore this opportunity in confidence, please get in touch directly.

title: Investment Corporate Actions Representative III/Associate Analyst
description: **Job Description** **Corporate Actions Analyst – Income \& Class Actions.** **Location: Dublin** Perform daily tasks associated with the processing and reconciliation of voluntary and mandatory corporate actions, as well as research Class Action cases. Process corporate actions promptly and correctly with policies, procedures, and regulations. Interact with various groups across the organization as well as external vendors and business partners. The **Expertise** We’re Looking For * 1\-2 years of relevant Financial Services experience. * Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions processing experience preferable. * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience. The **Purpose** of Your Role * Conduct and keep track of corporate action activities. * Conduct processing of all types of corporate action events, both voluntary and mandatory. * Facilitate the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions and provide daily cash estimates. * Coordinate corporate action executions, ensure the collection of income to the funds, resolve position differences, and facilitate securities lending activities. * Assist the team with corporate action research and process tasks related to assets under management. * Collaborate with stakeholders on issues and continuously follow up to resolve them. * Escalate high\-risk items or seek assistance to mitigate loss. * Develop the knowledge required to grasp product and processing rules while working under the direction of more experienced colleagues. * Display a strong sense of teamwork. * Explore and communicate new ways of improving existing processes. The **Skills** You Bring * Your knowledge of the key elements of Corporate Actions processing * Your knowledge of the Custody industry, Fund Accounting and Mutual Fund Operations * Your ability to manage multiple tasks effectively * Your ability to identify and escalate high risk items * Your strong written and verbal communications skills * Your time management skills * Your analytical and problem\-solving skills * Your Innovative mindset – you look for new ways to improve current processes and develop creative solutions. The **Value** You Deliver * Responsibility for high quality processing of Mandatory and Voluntary corporate actions * Managing the communication of complex corporate actions to internal stakeholders * Interacting with many groups within the Fidelity organization and effectively representing the business to customers and business partners * Escalating issues appropriately * Demonstrating a strong team\-work ethic How Your Work **Impacts** the Organization The Global Middle Office acts as a servicing centre for FMR Co.'s trading desks globally, facilitating the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions with brokers and custodian banks, and providing daily cash estimates to Trading. It is also responsible for coordinating corporate action executions, ensuring the collection of income to the Funds, resolving position differences and facilitating securities lending activities. **Company Overview** At Fidelity, we are focused on making our financial expertise broadly accessible and effective in helping people live the lives they want. We are a privately held company that places a high degree of value in creating and nurturing a work environment that attracts the best talent and reflects our commitment to our associates. Fidelity established an Ireland presence in 1996 and we currently employ 1000 staff across IT and shared services, with offices in Dublin (City West) and Galway (Parkmore East). For information about working at Fidelity, visit www.fidelityinvestments.ie. Fidelity Investments is an equal opportunity employer. **Category** Investment Operations

title: Junior Brand Accountant
description: **Junior Brand Accountant** **REPORTS TO:** Senior Commercial Accountant **DIRECT REPORTS:** None **ABOUT RICHMOND** At Richmond, we don’t just build brands—we ignite them. As Ireland’s leading trend\-spotting premium brand builder, we’ve been shaping the future since 1992\. Our mantra is simple: *Where Great People Meet Great Brands*. We don’t just hire employees; we bring on bold, agile, and energetic innovators who thrive on shaking up the status quo. We're not just about distributing products—we’re about disrupting the market, challenging the norms, and launching game\-changing ideas and products that leave a lasting impact. **JOB DESCRIPTION** The Junior Accountant role will support the finance function across a number of brands within our portfolio. Reporting directly to the Senior Commercial accountant, this position will assist with financial reporting, commercial pricing analysis, and investment management activities, while ensuring accurate financial records and timely reporting. The role offers an excellent opportunity to develop accounting and commercial finance experience within a dynamic multi\-brand environment. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** * Sales Reporting, Analytics \& IT Maintenance * Preparation of information for monthly/quarterly review meetings * Recharging Of Brand Marketing and Promotional Expenses * Assisting with Month End accounts \& Management Accounting Tasks * Assisting Commercial Accounting Team * Budgeting Preparation * Ad hoc project work **KNOWLEDGE SKILLS \& EXPERIENCE** * Part Qualified or must have a relevant College Degree * The Candidate Must Have the Intention of Completing Professional Accounting Exams * Must Have Excellent Verbal and Interpersonal Skills * Self\-Motivated, Team Player and Ambitious * Good Analytical Skills \& Attention to detail * Ability to prioritise responsibilities and manage deadlines * Strong IT Skills (Especially Excel)

title: Junior Project Coordinator to Recruitment
description: **Junior Project Coordinator – Changing Careers?** **Seeking a new direction this summer?** Join the Archer team as a Recruitment Consultant and build lasting relationships with key clients and top IT talent across Ireland and Europe. **Why Choose Archer?** * **Limitless Earnings:** Enjoy a performance\-based environment with massive earning potential. * **Career Elevation:** Determine your own progression in a clear, meritocratic environment. * **Top\-Tier Training:** Receive full mentorship, tailored 1\-to\-1 training, and access to our Leadership Academy. As an Associate Recruitment Executive, you will take full ownership of a specific technology market. You will manage the end\-to\-end recruitment process, consult with clients, and deliver high\-calibre talent. **What We Are Looking For:** * **2\+ years** in professional services, ideally in a Project Coordinator/Management role. * Experience in a client\- or customer\-facing environment is a major plus. * Strong communication and relationship\-building skills. **Compensation \& Benefits:** * **Base Salary:** DOE (Depending on Experience) * **Year 1 Total Comp:** €47,000 (Base \+ Bonus \& Commission) * **Year 2:** €70,000\+, **Year 3:** €100k\+ * **Location:** Ideal Dublin City Centre location **Ready to launch your new career?** For more information and insights, contact **Saoirse Lawton** at **\+353 (0\)1 6498517** .

title: Kitchen Porter
description: **Join the Heart of the Kitchen as a Kitchen Porter at Carluccio’s!** **About Us:** At Carluccio’s, we believe that every great dish begins with a clean and organised kitchen. Our passion for authentic Italian cuisine is matched by our commitment to maintaining the highest standards of cleanliness and efficiency. We’re looking for a dedicated Kitchen Porter to join our team and keep our kitchen running smoothly. **The Role:** As a Kitchen Porter at Carluccio’s, you’ll play a crucial role in supporting our chefs and ensuring that the kitchen is always clean, safe, and well\-organised. Here’s what you’ll be doing: * Keep the Kitchen Spotless: Maintain cleanliness in the kitchen by washing dishes, utensils, and equipment, and ensuring work surfaces are clean and sanitary. * Support the Team: Assist chefs and kitchen staff with basic food preparation tasks, and help with organising and storing deliveries. * Manage Waste: Properly dispose of waste and recycling, keeping the kitchen area tidy and free of hazards. * Stock and Organize: Help with the organisation and restocking of ingredients and supplies, making sure the kitchen is always ready for service. * Ensure Safety: Follow all health and safety guidelines to ensure a safe working environment for everyone in the kitchen. What’s In It For You? We’re Not Just Offering a Job; We’re Offering a Career Full Of Excitement And Growth * Feast on 50% Off your total bill for you and 5 friends across all our brands—because great food should be shared! * Unlock Exclusive Discounts on thousands of online and high\-street retailers, plus restaurants, through our BRG Spark App. * Stock Up on Favourites: Enjoy 20% off at Carluccio’s retail gift shop \& deli. * Get Paid When You Need It with Wage Stream, offering access to advanced pay. * Refer Friends and Earn with our bonus scheme. * Skyrocket Your Career: Excellent opportunities for career development across our diverse brand portfolio. * Work Your Way: Enjoy flexible working opportunities that suit your lifestyle. This is your chance to be an essential part of our kitchen team, where every contribution is valued. If you’re ready to start your career in hospitality or take the next step, join us at Carluccio’s as a Kitchen Porter and become part of our family! **Boparan Restaurant Group (BRG)** is a growing hospitality group committed to our goal of striving to become the Best Restaurant Group in everything we do. We’re obsessed with building credibility, going above and beyond, and delivering a memorable guest experience and an amazing working environment. **At BRG, We Live By Our Core Values** ✅ **Honest** – Acting with integrity in everything we do. ✅ **Hardworking** – Giving our best, every day. ✅ **Hungry** – Always striving for growth and excellence. ✅ **Heart** – Caring deeply about our people, our guests, and our communities. Carluccio's on Dawson Street offers a relaxed, authentic Italian dining experience with a menu featuring classic dishes made from fresh, quality ingredients. **Skills** Fast\-Paced Experience

title: Lawyer - Licensing
description: **Job Title: Lawyer \- Licensing** **Department: Litigation and Investigations** **Reporting to: Head of Litigation and Investigations** **Duration: Permanent** The Licensing lawyer will support the firm’s licensing and regulatory practice across the retail and hospitality sectors, by advising clients on licensing requirements, preparing and presenting Court applications and liaising with relevant regulatory authorities. This role involves working closely with partners and solicitors to deliver technical, commercial, and client\-focused licensing support across a broad client base. **The Role** In this role you will be responsible for * Advising clients on licensing requirements, processes, and compliance obligations across relevant sectors. * Preparing and presenting Court applications, reviewing, and managing licence applications, renewals, variations, and transfers on behalf of clients. * Drafting correspondences, submissions, and supporting documentation to regulatory authorities. * Liaising with regulators, advisers, and third parties to progress applications and resolve issues. * Tracking licence conditions, renewal dates, and compliance obligations for clients. * Conducting legal and regulatory research to support advisory work across various sectors. * Maintaining accurate client files in line with confidentiality and risk\-management procedures. * Supporting client relationship management and, where appropriate, business development initiatives. **The Person** * You have experience working in licensing, regulatory, or legal support within a law firm or professional services environment. * You are comfortable with preparing and presenting licensing applications and dealing with regulatory authorities. * You are familiarity with Irish and/or EU regulatory frameworks relevant to the firm’s clients. * You have a strong academic or professional background in law, compliance, or a related discipline. * You have prior experience in a top\-tier or mid\-tier law firm. * You have experience in transactional or advisory work involving retail and hospitality. **About us** William Fry LLP is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London, Silicon Valley and New York. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and over 500 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry is an equal opportunity Employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity by the Irish Centre for Diversity. Please let us know if you require specific arrangements during the recruitment process. We'll do everything we can to support you. All information disclosed will be kept confidential.

title: Lawyer - Property
description: Role: Lawyer – Property Reporting to: Property Partner The successful candidate will be joining an innovative and market leading firm with a growing practice who will be involved in all aspects of Commercial Real Estate matters. Our Property Department is recognised as outstanding in Ireland and is consistently rated as a tier 1 practice by leading legal directories. This innovative and hugely experienced team has been heavily involved in the majority of landmark real estate transactions in Ireland in the past 12 months both direct property investment and real estate loan portfolio sales. Our clients include large institutional investors, international private equity funds, receivers, public authorities and major Irish, UK and US retailers. **The Role:** * Manage end\-to\-end commercial real estate transactions including high value acquisitions, disposals and financings. * Draft, review, and negotiate complex contracts, leases, and ancillary legal documentation to safeguard client interests. * Advise clients on strategic property matters, including regulatory compliance, and risk mitigation. * Lead due diligence processes, ensuring accuracy and efficiency. * Develop and nurture client relationships, acting as a trusted advisor. * Stay abreast of market trends and legislative changes to provide proactive, commercially focused advice. * Contribute to business development initiatives, including thought leadership, client pitches, and networking events. * Mentor and support junior team members, fostering a culture of excellence and continuous learning. **The Person** * You will have between 3 and 7 years’ post qualification experience. * You have strong academic background. * You have strong technical and drafting skills and a good eye for detail. * You are commercially focused. * You have excellent organisational skills and a proven ability to manage multiple priorities and ensure that deadlines are consistently met. * You have the ability to show a high level of initiative but can effectively work as part of a team. **About us** William Fry LLP is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London, San Francisco, Silicon Valley and New York. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and over 460 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry is an equal opportunity Employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity by the Irish Centre for Diversity. Please let us know if you require specific arrangements during the recruitment process. We'll do everything we can to support you. All information disclosed will be kept confidential.

title: Loss Prevention Officer
description: TJX Europe At TJX Europe, every day brings new opportunities for growth, exploration, and achievement. You’ll be part of our vibrant team that embraces diversity, fosters collaboration, and prioritises your development. Whether you’re working in our Distribution Centers, Corporate Offices, or Retail Stores—TK Maxx \& Homesense, you’ll find abundant opportunities to learn, thrive, and make an impact. Come join our TJX team—a Fortune 100 company and the world’s leading off\-price retailer. **Job Description:** **Could you be one of our Loss Prevention Officers?** As the world's leading off\-price retailer of clothing and homeware, we're all about putting our customers first. With thousands of shops worldwide, we aim to provide a positive and memorable experience for all. Working as a **Loss Prevention Officer**, you'll help to deliver our goal of keeping our customers, associates and assets safe. Experience of being in a customer facing role, within a fast\-paced retail environment or security guard role is ideal but not essential. **In this role:** You will be supporting a positive customer experience within our stores by being a visible, friendly, approachable presence at our entrance doors. You will be focused on deterring loss on the salesfloor whilst acting as an ambassador for the loss prevention function. You will work closely with the store team to maintain security of our product and together you will aim to deliver effective conflict resolution when required. You will need to demonstrate your ability to remain calm and professional under pressure. You will also be self\-motivated, resilient and driven to make a difference with a natural ability to communicate. **In return, you'll get an in\-depth programme of training when you start.** **Are we right for you?** We're all about helping you develop yourself. The skills you'll learn working within Loss Prevention will help you be at your best. Plus, you'll be surrounded by the support of our fun and caring team. Also as a TJX Europe associate, you'll enjoy such things as: Competitive Salary Uniform Provided Comprehensive package of benefits to support your mental, physical and financial wellbeing such as access to an employee assistance program, associate discount and service awards Join us, and you’ll be a part of our thriving, growing European business filled with opportunities for growth and recognition. We’re proud of our culture, we work hard, have lots of fun, and give back to many causes we care about. It will be a role you won't just like but love. As proud as we are of our past success, it’s our future that excites us most. We strive to provide opportunities for growth, recognition and a competitive salary and benefits package. Share our determination to think bolder and bigger, and be part of our future. We consider all applicants for employment without regard to age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnerships, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief and/or sexual orientation.**Address:** Unit 1 Main Street The Courtyard Shopping Centre**Location:** EUR TK Maxx IE Store 371 \- Newbridge

title: MULTIFUNCTIONAL SALES ASSOCIATE (30 Hours, Fix Term, Henry St.)
description: At MANGO we style everything we do with passion. Founded in Barcelona and with presence in over 120 countries, we inspire the world with creativity, innovation and authenticity. Our multicultural team, is the driving force behind our success, connecting our unique style with people all over the world. **YOUR NEW POSITION:** For our store in **Dublin\-Henry St.** , we are looking for a Multifunctional Sales Associate to join our team. * **30 hours per week** --------------------- * **Fix Term** ------------ * **Monday to Sunday** -------------------- The Multifunctional Sales Associate is responsible for ensuring the best customer experience, contributing to increasing store sales, and maintaining Mango's image and customer service standards, while also performing cashier duties and supporting stockroom operations. **YOUR MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Customer Experience Excellence: Provide exceptional service to customers, ensuring their needs are met and they have a positive shopping experience that reflects Mango's brand values. * Sales Contribution: Actively contribute to increasing store sales by engaging with customers, understanding their preferences, and recommending products that meet their needs. * Cashier Duties: Efficiently handle cashier responsibilities, including processing transactions, managing returns and exchanges, and ensuring accurate cash handling and register balancing. * Stockroom Support: Assist in stockroom operations, including receiving, organizing, and replenishing merchandise, ensuring inventory accuracy and timely distribution to the sales floor. * Image and Service Standards Maintenance: Uphold Mango's image and customer service standards, ensuring the store is well\-presented and service delivery is consistent and professional. * Product Knowledge Utilization: Utilize product knowledge to assist customers effectively, highlighting key features and benefits to drive interest and sales. * Team Collaboration: Work collaboratively with the store team to support business objectives, sharing insights and feedback to enhance the overall customer experience. **ABOUT YOU:** The ideal candidate is customer\-oriented, proactive, and responsible, with the ability to multitask across sales, cashier, and stockroom functions. They should possess the skills to engage customers effectively, contribute to sales growth, and maintain high standards of service and presentation. **YOUR BENEFITS:** * As a member of the MANGO team you’ll get a discount on all our lines, so that you’ll always be wearing the latest! * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP): confidential helpline, money concerns, general well\-being and legal advice. * At MANGO, we invest in your personal and professional development. Access a wide range of training courses, personalized mentoring, continuous development programs and internal promotion opportunities that will drive you towards. success. * Think big! MANGO offers you international opportunities in over 120 markets for you to broaden your horizons and grow with us globally. At MANGO, we believe in an inclusive culture where creativity and innovation inspire us to take fashion further, therefore, we are committed to providing equal opportunities for all, valuing each person’s authenticity. **Craft your own story** ========================

title: Mainframe Application Developer
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** U.S. Bank is seeking a **Mainframe COBOL / CICS** **DB2** **Application Developer** with proven full SDLC experience to contribute toward the success of our technology initiatives. In this role you will be part of an Agile, DevOps team established to build, enhance and support our Merchant Acquiring System. You will consult with product owners, development leads and business owners on requirement gathering / refinement. You will be responsible for development, deployment and production support of a large, complex application. **As an Application Developer you will:** * Participate in all aspects of SDLC; design, coding, testing, implementation, and documentation of solutions of moderate complexity, significance, and risk. * Follow best practices of software development including, following coding standards, detailed and thorough testing, detailed documentation, and code\-reviews. * Create peer reviewed comprehensive technical designs, unit test plans and test cases. * Collaborate in team agile ceremonies, Daily Stand\-ups, refinement sessions, sprint planning. Provide clear and valuable input to discussions. Support colleagues, developers, test engineers and product owners. * Review, investigate and answer internal customer’s queries. * Troubleshoot and resolve production issues. * Join a 24x7 On\-Call rota, periodically providing out of hours support for our mainframe\-based applications. **Basic Qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree, or equivalent work experience. * At least five years of experience with developing and implementing applications. **The following skills and proven experience with are required:** * mainframe COBOL / CICS * JCL, Batch Procedures and Parms * DB2 / SQL * IBM IDz * TSO * Batch scheduler e.g. Ca7 **The following skills and proven experience with are advantageous:** * working within an agile methodology * IBM File Manager * VSAM * GIT * ServiceNow or other ITSM * Jira or other agile tool * Visual Studio * CoPilot or other AI development tools Prior knowledge of the payment industry or merchant acquiring is not required but is a distinct advantage. **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. title: Maintenance Planner
description: **Job Description :** **Maintenance****Planner** **Littleconnell****Brewery,****Co.Kildare** **Permanent****Contract** **Closing Date: 14****th****June** **Maintenance****Planner****\- €****56,700 \- €69,300****plus****competitive benefits package with pension, shares scheme and bonus.** *This is a day shift role working Monday – Friday.* **Join our Supply Chain and Manufacturing team.** Our Supply Chain and Manufacturing team are problem\-solvers, sourcing, producing, and delivering our much\-loved brands to delight customers around the globe. Over half of Diageo’s 30,000\+ people work in Supply Chain and Manufacturing. It’s an intricate and sophisticated operation that’s the product of logistical, manufacturing, and technical collaborations. We are proud to be a global leader in beverage alcohol with an exceptional portfolio of brands, including Guinness, Johnnie Walker, Baileys, Smirnoff, Tanqueray, and many more. A forward\-thinking €200 million investment has brought an innovative, carbon\-neutral brewery to life in Newbridge, Co. Kildare. This best\-in\-class facility is Ireland’s second\-largest brewing operation and a powerhouse of sustainability, running entirely on renewable energy. It’s where iconic beers like Rockshore, Harp, Smithwick’s, and Kilkenny are crafted for a new generation. **About the Role** We are seeking a Maintenance Planner to ensure high standards of maintenance and asset care across our brewery, utilities, and water treatment facilities. Working in a semi\-autonomous environment, you’ll play a key role in end\-to\-end maintenance planning and execution, managing SAP processes and driving reliability, performance, and continuous improvement across site operations. Key Responsibilities: * Plan and schedule maintenance activities, coordinating labour, materials, and resources effectively * Manage SAP processes, including maintenance plans, materials setup, and system updates (BOMs, equipment, functional locations) * Monitor and report on KPIs such as equipment reliability and efficiency, driving continuous improvement * Control maintenance costs and optimize spare parts inventory levels * Coordinate maintenance shutdowns and lead integration between operations and maintenance teams * Support root cause analysis and contribute to performance improvements and risk reduction * Ensure compliance with health, safety, and hygiene standards, including contractor supervision **About you** You’ll be a proactive and organised Maintenance Planner with a strong technical background and a passion for driving performance and reliability. You’ll be comfortable working both independently and as part of a team, with the ability to prioritise effectively and solve problems in a fast\-paced environment. **What****You’ll****Bring:** * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage and prioritise multiple tasks * Good understanding of preventative and reliability\-centred maintenance techniques * Knowledge of asset performance, maintenance strategy, and root cause problem solving * Experience using SAP\-PM for planning, scheduling, and maintenance execution * Basic financial awareness, including maintenance costs and budgeting principles * FMCG experience is advantageous but not essential **Working with us** Our purpose is to celebrate life, every day, everywhere. And creating an inclusive culture, where everyone feels valued and that they can belong, is a crucial part of this. We embrace diversity in the broadest possible sense. This means that you’ll be welcomed and celebrated for who you are just by being you. You’ll be part of and help build and champion an inclusive culture that celebrates people of different gender, ethnicity, ability, age, sexual orientation, social class, educational backgrounds, experiences, mindsets, and more. Our ambition is to create the best performing, most trusted and respected consumer products companies in the world. Join us and help transform our business as we take our brands to the next level and build new ones as part of shaping the next generation of celebrations for consumers around the world. Feel inspired? Then this may be the opportunity for you! *If you require a reasonable adjustment, please ensure that you capture this information when you**submit**your application.* **Worker Type :** Regular**Primary Location:** Littleconnell Brewery**Additional Locations :** **Job Posting Start Date :** 2026\-05\-29

title: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer
description: **Full Time Permanent Role** **Citywest Office (Hybrid) or UK (Remote)** **The Role** Uniphar are embarking on an ambitious, multi\-year transformation program and are seeking a Dynamics 365 CE Developer to support our teams across our transformation initiatives. We are seeking an experienced and dynamic developer to join our team. The ideal candidate will possess a strong background in Dynamics 365 Customer Service. The Dynamics 365 Developer will play a crucial role in supporting \& enhancing existing functionality along with building out greenfield Dynamics 365 Customer Services solution as we continue to grow and innovate. **Key Responsibilities** Technical Experience * Configuring Dynamics 365 CE solutions, specifically in Customer Service environments. * Operate effectively within a Managed D365 environment * Design, build and maintain Power Automate flows to streamline and automate business processes. * Deploy solutions using CI/CD pipelines (manual deployment to test \& production environments will not be allowed) * Manage and maintain Git\-based source control, ensuring proper versioning and collaboration practices * Support the CRM Architect by contributing to documentation, configuration oversight, and integrations. Dynamics CRM Experience * Configure CRM components including but not limited to Tables, Views, Forms, Business Process Flows, Security Roles, and Workflows. * Ability to develop plugins and JavaScript libraries * Support development of and maintaining Canvas Apps where needed. * Maintain up\-to\-date skills on new features and solutions. **Project Responsibilities** * Working within the guidelines and to the objectives defined by any project or program currently running. * Working within an Agile sprint structure * Prioritise work tasks and report updates and status reports to any relevant project streams. **Requirements** * 5\+ years Dynamics 365 development experience, preferably with Customer Engagement (CE/CRM) * Strong knowledge of Dynamics 365 Customer Service \& Power Platform * Ability to design innovative, re\-usable solutions and components. * Experience of building plugins, workflows, Power Automate flows, and Logic and Canvas apps. * Experience of designing integrations in Azure * Experience with ALM tools (e.g., Git for Enterprise, Azure Devops). * Strong troubleshooting skills in a cloud and solutions architecture context. * Ability to collaborate effectively with cross\-functional teams. * Proven ability to analyse complex technical issues and implement solutions based on industry best practices * Experience of building web apps in Azure or SAP integration is an advantage * Good working experience with SaaS solutions and integrations. **Uniphar – Who We Are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialised services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process. *Please note that Uniphar is an equal\-opportunity employer; we do not discriminate and welcome all responses.* \#uniphargroup \#unipharjobs

title: Operational Security Analyst (IT & Payment Systems)
description: Indra is one of the leading global technology and consulting companies and the technological partner for core business operations of its customers worldwide. It is a world\-leader in providing proprietary solutions in specific segments in Transport and Defence markets, and the leading firm in Digital Transformation Consultancy and Information Technologies in Spain and Latin America through its affiliate Minsait. Its business model is based on a comprehensive range of proprietary products, with a high\-value focus and with a high innovation component. In the 2024 financial year, Indra achieved revenue of € 4\.84 billion, 60,000\+ employees, a local presence in 46 countries and business operations in over 140 countries. As the technological partner for its customers' key operations, Indra is at the core of their business, and Indra's four values guide everything we do: **Innovation** \- Our capacity for innovation, cutting\-edge solutions, and specialised team of professionals enable us to drive a safer, more connected future through technology. **Trust** \- We work with strength, commitment, and reliability, delivering quality solutions to build trust with customers, employees, partners, investors, and society. **Connection** \- We harness the power of collaboration, connect ideas and solutions, and adapt to our customers' needs, supporting them on the path to a better future. **Foresight** \- We anticipate future needs to make the world safer and more connected, transforming our experience and knowledge into solutions for a better tomorrow. **Role Overview** We are seeking an Operational Security Analyst to support the day\-to\-day delivery of information security across our IT, cloud, and payment environments. This role sits within the operations function and is focused on embedding security into live services, ensuring controls are effective, risks are understood, and security practices are consistently applied across engineering and operational teams. The successful candidate will work closely with service delivery, infrastructure, and third\-party providers to support a secure, compliant, and resilient operating environment, particularly within regulated payment (PCI DSS / P2PE) contexts. **Key Responsibilities** * Operational Security \& Service Support * Act as a point of contact for operational security matters, supporting service delivery across cloud, infrastructure, and payment environments * Support the implementation and ongoing operation of security controls across AWS, on\-prem infrastructure, and end\-user environments * Work closely with engineering and operations teams to ensure security is embedded into day\-to\-day activities **Security Operations \& Monitoring** * Support SIEM monitoring and alert triage, identifying and responding to potential security events * Assist in security event investigation and escalation, ensuring appropriate response and documentation * Contribute to improving monitoring coverage and detection capability **Risk \& Vulnerability Management** * Assist in identifying, assessing, and tracking information security risks in collaboration with risk owners * Support vulnerability management processes, including: * Scanning and identification * Prioritisation and remediation tracking * Reporting and follow\-up **Payment Security (PCI / P2PE)** * Support operational security controls within Cardholder Data Environments (CDE) * Assist in maintaining PCI DSS compliance, including: * Logging and monitoring * Access control reviews * Vulnerability management activities * Gain exposure to P2PE environments and device ecosystems, supporting secure operations **Security Controls \& Cryptography** * Assist in the operation of cryptographic controls, with awareness of: * Key management practices * Secure handling and storage * Support access control and identity management processes **Documentation, Standards \& Continuous Improvement** * Contribute to the development and maintenance of: Security procedures and Standards and runbooks * Promote security awareness across teams, ensuring policies are understood and followed * Support automation and continuous improvement initiatives within security operations **Third\-Party \& Compliance Support** * Assist with third\-party security reviews and control validation activities * Support audit and compliance processes, including evidence gathering for: PCI DSS and ISO 27001 * Contribute to maintaining a strong control and governance environment **Requirements** * Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cyber Security, or equivalent practical experience * 1\-3 years' experience in information security, IT operations, or a related technical role **Technical Skills** * Exposure to security operations, including SIEM monitoring or alert triage * Familiarity with vulnerability management tools and processes * Basic understanding of cloud platforms (e.g. AWS) and IT infrastructure **Core Security Knowledge** * Understanding of: Network security fundamentals, Identity and Access Management (IAM, MFA), Endpoint security principles, * Awareness of frameworks such as: ISO 27001, PCI DSS, NIS2 / DORA (desirable) **Desirable Experience** * Exposure to payment security environments (PCI DSS / P2PE) * Experience working in regulated or operational environments **Personal Attributes** * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Good documentation skills and attention to detail * Clear communicator, able to work across technical and non\-technical teams * Proactive mindset with a willingness to learn and take ownership * Comfortable working both independently and as part of a team in a fast\-paced environment **Benefits** * Holidays: 24 days per annum \+ Irish bank holidays (options to buy/sell days) * Pension \- 7% employee and 7% employer * Private medical insurance (including dental \& optical) * Flexible/hybrid working options * Charitable initiatives * Learning and development programs * Travel \& expense allowances * Innovative \& collaborative work environment *Indra is an equal employment opportunity employer. Applicants are considered without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability or other characteristics protected by law.*

title: People Systems & Analytics Specialist (Workday)
description: Clio is the global leader in legal AI technology, empowering legal professionals and law firms of every size to work smarter, faster, and more securely. We are transforming the legal experience for all by bettering the lives of legal professionals while increasing access to justice. **Summary:** We’re looking for a Workday People Systems \& Analytics Specialist to join our rapidly growing People Systems \& Analytics team in Dublin. This role is for someone who thrives in a rapid\-growth and high\-velocity environment. This is a role for someone who gets genuinely excited about making systems work better, digging into data, and helping a scaling organisation run more smoothly. **About The Team** Our People Systems \& Analytics team sits at the intersection of people, process and technology. We own and optimise the tools that power our People function, make sure our data is accurate and accessible, and help the wider business make better decisions through smart reporting and analytics. As Clio continues to scale, this team plays a bigger and bigger role in how we grow well. What you'll be working on **You'll be a core contributor to some genuinely interesting and high impact work, including:** * Playing a key role in the implementation of Workday, our HRIS, covering configuration, day\-to\-day support, change management, and helping employees get comfortable with the new system * Supporting testing across multiple Workday modules and acting as a knowledgeable partner to our People, IT and Finance teams as we roll things out * Owning documentation across our systems and processes, keeping things accurate and up to date throughout the full lifecycle of each system * Maintaining the data integrity and information security of our HRIS and broader People Systems * Creating and iterating on documentation for future\-state People processes, and proactively flagging areas where we can streamline or improve * Supporting our biannual system upgrades, including testing and assessing downstream impacts across the business * Building, maintaining and optimising dashboards and reporting practices that power our People Analytics strategy * Partnering with our People and Finance teams to extract and present data in a way that is clear, insightful and actually useful ****What you might bring**** * 2 to 3 years of experience in the People function of a fast\-growing tech company, with a strong focus on HR Systems Workday and People Analytics * A sharp eye for detail and strong organisational and auditing skills, you catch things others miss * A solid understanding of best practices in People and HR analytics * Experience building dashboards that take complex data and make it genuinely understandable and actionable for non\-technical stakeholders * Advanced spreadsheet skills in Google Sheets or Excel ****Bonus points for**** * A good For experienced individuals, we typically hire at or around the midpoint of the band. The top portion of the salary band is reserved for employees who demonstrate sustained high performance and impact at Clio. title: Podcast Producer
description: RTÉ is Ireland’s national public\-service media organisation. We are embarking upon an ambitious and exciting 5\-year transformation strategy to update the way we run the organisation. Our mission is to serve as an independent source of stories, news, information and entertainment for Ireland, reflecting the diversity of its people and providing experiences that enrich the lives of all. This sense of purpose motivates us to deliver to the highest standards in an environment that is collaborative, supportive, and engaging. Attracting and retaining the best talent is key in delivering to our audiences. We want our people to thrive in an environment that values their output. **Meet the Team** RTÉ Podcasts was created to engage with an audience aged under 45 which increasingly consumes media on non\-linear platforms. Our plan is to build habit\-forming podcasts which place RTÉ at the centre of this audience’s digital audio consumption. **About the role:** We are currently seeking to appoint two Podcast Producers to work across Entertainment and Sport. If successful in your application, you will:* Perform a central role in building RTÉ’s podcasting operation, informing its identity as it grows * Work closely with the Head of Podcasting in the process of developing new ideas * Initiate and create compelling, unmissable content for a range of platforms including digital audio, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube and RTÉ.ie * Oversee all aspects of a podcast's production from idea generation onwards \- to include set design, brand design, host and guest management * Manage each episode’s editing, recording, distribution and promotion on audio, video and text platforms, including social media * Be part of a creative team working to strict deadlines \- often in pressurised situations * Interact with people working across RTÉ and the independent sector in the production and promotion of RTÉ podcasts * Work with podcast platforms to increase the visibility and promotion of RTÉ’s podcasts **About you:** The podcasting department is looking to hire people who are innovative, tech\-literate, and editorially brave. Successful candidates for either of the above jobs will have:* An instinctive drive to connect with audiences and an understanding of the younger audience that is currently underserved by RTÉ * A strong sense of what makes a compelling host, guest, topic or format idea * A strong background in digital media production, with an ability to use images, video, audio and text to maximise the impact of RTÉ’s podcasts across various platforms – in particular on social media * An understanding of podcast formats and trends in digital audio * A readiness to learn new technologies as they emerge or become relevant to the work of the podcasting department * An ability to work constructively and positively in a fast\-moving production environment * Sound editorial judgement and the capacity to work closely and creatively with others * An understanding of how AI is shaping the media environment will be an advantage \- including both the challenges it poses and the opportunities it presents. **If you are interested in applying for this opportunity, please apply by submitting:*** your Curriculum Vitae * a cover letter (no more than 300 words), outlining your interest in the role, and suitability to its requirements. * and a paper (no more than 850 words) on one of the following topics:How I would build an Irish version of the Ringer Podcast Network, **or** How I would create an Irish version of the Shameless Podcast, and what other podcasts could be generated as spin offs from that. One appointment will be offered on a full\-time fixed term contract for a duration of two years. The second appointment will be made on a full\-time fixed purpose contract for a duration of 6 months. Both appointments will be made on the Radio Producer salary scale from €54,661 gross pa to €85,602 gross per annum. Closing date for receipt of applications is: **Friday 12th June 2026**. Cuirfear fáilte roimh iarratais ó dhaoine go bhfuil dearcadh dearfach acu i leith na Gaeilge. Applications are welcome from people who have a positive outlook to Irish. Diversity and Inclusion are at the heart of what we do – both on\-screen and behind the scenes. We are committed to building a more inclusive environment and tackling under\-representation while embedding inclusion at every stage of the employee journey. We welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds, without regard to gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, social experience, race and ethnicity, disability, civil and family status, religious belief and membership of the Traveller Community. We are eager to provide reasonable accommodations for potential and existing employees with any kind of disability. Should you wish to request accommodation for your interview, we encourage you to let us know when you apply.

title: Prep Chef
description: **Job Overview** **\*\* MUST HAVE A FULL DRIVING LICENSE\*\*** We are seeking a dedicated and experienced Prep Chef with a valid van driving licence to join our team. This dual role involves assisting in food preparation within our busy kitchen environment and ensuring timely delivery of supplies and meals. The ideal candidate will possess strong culinary skills, leadership qualities, and excellent organisational abilities to support our food service operations. This position offers an opportunity to work in a dynamic setting where teamwork, food safety, and customer satisfaction are paramount. **Duties** * Prepare ingredients and assist in the cooking of meals according to established recipes and standards * Supervise junior kitchen staff, ensuring food quality and safety protocols are maintained * Manage food production schedules to meet service deadlines efficiently * Oversee the organisation of kitchen stations, ensuring cleanliness and compliance with health regulations * Drive the van to deliver prepared food items or supplies to designated locations promptly and safely * Assist in inventory management, including stock rotation and ordering supplies as needed * Support the team during busy periods by providing leadership and maintaining high standards of hospitality and service * Ensure all food safety standards are adhered to throughout food preparation and delivery processes **Requirements** * Proven experience in food preparation, cooking, or kitchen environments, ideally within hospitality or restaurant settings * Valid van driving licence with a clean driving record * Strong knowledge of food safety regulations and best practices in food production * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to multitask effectively under pressure * Good communication skills to coordinate with team members and clients effectively * Previous experience in hospitality, restaurant work, or serving roles is advantageous but not essential This role offers an engaging environment for individuals passionate about culinary arts and customer service, with opportunities for professional growth within our organisation. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €14\.50\-€25\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company car * Employee discount * Food allowance * On\-site parking * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: Product Marketing Specialist
description: **Join beqom — where tech meets impact** beqom is a high\-growth B2B SaaS company that provides industry\-leading tools for pay equity and transparency, compensation, and performance management. Trusted by some of the world’s most respected companies, beqom enables HR and business leaders to navigate global compliance and make smarter pay decisions that attract, retain, and motivate top talent. Founded in Switzerland and serving clients worldwide, our powerful, enterprise\-ready products are fuelled by beqom pay intelligence. **Role Overview** As a Product Marketing Specialist at beqom, you’ll be at the heart of our commercial success — shaping how we position, launch, and enable the market around our products. Reporting to the Head of Product Marketing and working closely with Product, Marketing, Sales, and Services teams, you will own the tactical execution of product marketing strategy: from compelling messaging and go\-to\-market planning, to sales enablement and competitive positioning. This is a role for someone who can move seamlessly between strategic thinking and hands\-on delivery — and who brings a genuine curiosity about how AI can make that work sharper and faster. **What you'll be doing** **Positioning \& Messaging** * Work with the Head of Product Marketing to develop and execute product marketing strategies and plans for new and existing products, timed to business priorities. * Create compelling, differentiated product messaging tailored to distinct buyer groups, segments, and industry verticals. * Develop clear campaign briefs and collaborate with demand generation on execution. * Ensure consistency of messaging across all customer touchpoints — website, email, sales decks, and collateral. * Produce high\-quality sales assets including pitch decks, one\-pagers, playbooks, brochures, product videos, objection\-handling guides, and FAQs that support every stage of the sales cycle. * Maintain regular updates of competitive battlecards, objection\-handling guidance, and positioning materials in response to market shifts. * Define and evolve beqom’s USPs in collaboration with Product, ensuring they resonate with buyer needs and market dynamics. * Create monthly analysis and progress reports on product marketing activity, with clear metrics and KPIs. **Sales Enablement** * Design, coordinate, and deliver field enablement programmes that support product launches, messaging updates, and major GTM initiatives. * Partner with Product to roll out product updates in an actionable, field\-friendly format. * Champion a culture of continuous learning and field empowerment across the commercial organisation through sales accreditation programmes. * Develop, maintain, and evolve a central library of enablement content and resources. * Track adoption and impact of enablement programmes and iterate based on feedback and performance data. **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Act as a trusted partner to commercial colleagues, ensuring consistent articulation of product value across all touchpoints. * Collaborate with Product Management, Marketing, Sales, and other teams to ensure successful product launches and seamless market integration. * Work in close alignment with the Growth Marketing team to coordinate campaigns, sales plays, and promotional activities that build product awareness and generate leads in targeted markets. * Partner with Sales and Product Management to develop bespoke materials that support growth opportunities with major clients. **Skills \& Experience** **What you'll bring** * Proven experience in product marketing or a closely related role in B2B tech, with a strong understanding of SaaS buyer journeys. * Demonstrable experience crafting messaging and supporting go\-to\-market for portfolio products. * Experience with product launches and go\-to\-market planning. * Experience supporting software product lifecycle communications including general availability and release communications. * Strong customer\-centric mindset — able to identify pain points and translate them into compelling marketing strategies. * Proven stakeholder management skills, including working with senior leadership and cross\-functional teams. * Strategic thinker with strong execution skills — you can move from idea to action with clarity and pace. * An AI\-first mindset, actively exploring how GenAI tools can improve and accelerate your work. * Excellent project management and organisational skills, with strong attention to detail. * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience). **Bonus points if you have** * Experience in the HR tech space or selling into HR and Finance buyers. * Fluent in French * Familiarity with compensation management, pay equity, or performance management solutions.
